displayTitle: The Direct VT Window: 600 mm and 30 Degrees

beats.narration: Direct visual testing means an unbroken optical path from the surface to your eye. But that view only counts inside a specific window.

payload.title: THE DIRECT WINDOW
payload.sub: The distance and angle that make a direct view count.

beats.narration: Here's the window: eye within 600 millimeters — that's 24 inches — and a line of sight at least 30 degrees to the surface.

payload.heading: The direct viewing window
payload.note: EYE WITHIN 600 MM · ANGLE AT LEAST 30°

beats.narration: Past 600 millimeters, fine detail drops below what your eye can resolve. Below 30 degrees, the view foreshortens — a round pit flattens into a sliver.
Aids can help — a mirror sees around corners, a magnifier enlarges detail, a telescope reaches high nameplates — and the optical path stays unbroken.

payload.heading: Aids that keep the path unbroken

beats.narration: Inside the window, you can trust your eye. So remember: closer and squarer always beats farther and flatter.

payload.sub: Meet the distance and angle before you trust the view.
